name: Marinade for Charcoaled Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the ingredients in a jar and shake well.
2. Using a large fork, poke holes all over the roast.
3. Place the roast and marinade mixture into a large Ziploc bag or a flat pan, making sure to squeeze out any excess air so everything is thoroughly coated with the marinade.
4. Refrigerate the marinated roast for at least 2 days.
5. Build a medium fire in the smoker, omitting the water pan.
6. Place the roast on the grill and cook for about 20 minutes per side until it reaches your desired doneness (medium).
7. Check for doneness and continue cooking if necessary.
8. The roast should be dark brown on the outside and very tender inside.
I made a medium fire in the bottom of the smoker and left out the water pan and placed the roast on the grill for about 20 minutes per side. It is supposed to be medium, not well done. You'll have to check for doneness then and if not done to your taste, cook longer. It is FANTASTIC!! Dark brown on the outside and very tender on the inside.
I also used the same marinade, doubled, and marinated a whole sirloin tip in a huge stainless steel bowl and smoked it with the water pan; it was great too for serving a crowd!
